title: Distributed Product Lifecycle Platform
goal: Transform protoLabs from a single-instance task runner into a distributed product creation platform — from idea to production. CRDT-synced operational state (features, projects, notes, calendar, todos, settings) across multiple Automaker instances on Tailscale VPN, with an agentic Stage-Gate lifecycle (8 automated gates), cross-instance work-stealing, Ava Mesh orchestration, DORA metrics as auto-regulation control signals, and outcome-driven features (success metrics, kill conditions, hypotheses). The full lifecycle flywheel: signals → gates → execution → promotion → reflection → learning → better signals.

milestones.title: Foundation
milestones.description: Core CRDT infrastructure: proto.config loader, libs/crdt workspace package, Automerge document management, and WebSocket sync adapter for Tailscale.

phases.name: proto-config-loader
phases.title: proto.config Loader
phases.description: Create proto.config.yaml schema and loader in libs/platform. Implements layered config resolution: proto.config.yaml (git-tracked shared defaults) -> .automaker/settings.json (instance-local overrides) -> environment variables. Defines hive identity, instance registry, sync port, assignment strategy, and shared defaults. Loader returns effective config for the current instance.
acceptanceCriteria: ProtoConfig TypeScript types defined with hive, defaults, instances, and assignment sections
loadProtoConfig() reads YAML, merges with instance-local settings and env vars
Missing proto.config.yaml returns null (single-instance mode continues to work)
Instance identity resolved from hostname or explicit config
Unit tests for layered merge logic

phases.name: libs-crdt-workspace-package
phases.title: libs/crdt Workspace Package
phases.description: Create new workspace package @protolabsai/crdt. Wraps Automerge 3 + automerge-repo with a CRDTStore class that manages Automerge documents by domain (features, projects, notes, config). Provides document creation, change, subscribe, and sync lifecycle. Handles persistence to .automaker/crdt/ directory. Includes WebSocket sync adapter configured for Tailscale peer connections.
acceptanceCriteria: CRDTStore class creates and manages Automerge documents by domain name
change() method applies mutations and emits local change events
subscribe() method fires callbacks on local and remote changes
WebSocket sync adapter connects to peer via Tailscale IP:port
Persistence adapter saves/loads Automerge binary to .automaker/crdt/
Two-node integration test: change on node A appears on node B within 200ms
Conflict test: concurrent field updates on same document merge correctly
Package builds and passes typecheck

phases.name: sync-server-and-leader-election
phases.title: Sync Server and Leader Election
phases.description: Implement sync server lifecycle within the Automaker server process. The instance marked as role:primary in proto.config.yaml starts the automerge-repo-sync-server on the configured syncPort. If the primary goes down, the next instance by priority promotes itself. Heartbeat protocol using HivemindPeer types. Integrates with existing server startup/shutdown lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: Primary instance starts sync server on configured port
Worker instances connect as clients to primary's sync server
Heartbeat protocol updates HivemindPeer.lastSeen on each beat
Peers marked offline after peerTtlMs without heartbeat
Leader election: if primary unreachable, next instance by config order promotes
Graceful shutdown disconnects sync and announces departure
Health endpoint includes sync status and peer list

milestones.title: Feature Sync
milestones.description: Implement AutomergeFeatureStore backed by CRDT documents, replacing filesystem reads/writes for feature data. Events propagate across instances via EventBus.broadcast().

phases.name: automergefeaturestore-implementation
phases.title: AutomergeFeatureStore Implementation
phases.description: Implement the FeatureStore interface using CRDTStore as the backing store. Features document is a Record<featureId, Feature>. All reads come from in-memory Automerge doc (instant, no disk I/O). All writes use CRDTStore.change() which handles CRDT mutation, persistence, and sync. Maintains backward compatibility: if CRDT is disabled (no proto.config), falls back to existing FeatureLoader.
acceptanceCriteria: AutomergeFeatureStore implements FeatureStore interface
getAll() reads from Automerge doc (in-memory, no disk I/O)
create/update/delete use CRDTStore.change() for mutations
claim() uses CRDT atomic update with instanceId
Remote changes from peers trigger feature:updated events via EventBus
Fallback to FeatureLoader when proto.config is absent
Unit tests for all FeatureStore methods against Automerge doc
Integration test: feature created on instance A appears on instance B

phases.name: eventbus-crdt-bridge
phases.title: EventBus CRDT Bridge
phases.description: Wire EventBus.broadcast() to emit events across instances via CRDT change subscriptions. When a remote peer changes a feature, the local EventBus emits the corresponding event (feature:updated, feature:status-changed, etc.) so the UI and auto-mode react as if the change were local. This is the glue between CRDT sync and the existing event-driven architecture.
acceptanceCriteria: broadcast() publishes events to CRDT ephemeral messages when sync is active
Remote CRDT changes trigger local EventBus.emit() with correct event type
Feature status changes from peers trigger feature:status-changed events
UI receives WebSocket events for remote changes (board updates in real-time)
Auto-mode reacts to remote feature status changes (dep resolution, work stealing)
No duplicate events when change originates locally

milestones.title: Projects, Notes, and Settings Sync
milestones.description: Extend CRDT sync to projects, notes workspace, and shared settings. Same pattern as features — Automerge documents per domain, change subscriptions, event propagation.

phases.name: project-sync
phases.title: Project Sync
phases.description: Create Automerge document for projects (Record<slug, Project>). ProjectService reads/writes via CRDTStore. Project ceremonies (future) will sync as project sub-documents. PRD and milestone markdown content stored as Automerge text fields for future rich-text CRDT.
acceptanceCriteria: Projects document syncs across instances
Project created on instance A visible on instance B
Project updates (status, milestones) propagate in real-time
Backward compatible: works without CRDT (falls back to filesystem)

phases.name: notes-workspace-sync
phases.title: Notes Workspace Sync
phases.description: Sync NotesWorkspace JSON (tab metadata, tab order, active tab, content as HTML string) via Automerge document. This is workspace-level sync, not character-level rich-text CRDT — the full HTML content string is an LWW field. Rich-text CRDT via @automerge/prosemirror is deferred to Phase 5.
acceptanceCriteria: Notes workspace syncs across instances (tabs, order, content)
Tab created on instance A appears on instance B
Content updates propagate (last-write-wins at HTML string level)
workspaceVersion counter resolves to max across instances

phases.name: shared-settings-sync
phases.title: Shared Settings Sync
phases.description: Create a shared-settings Automerge document for configuration that should propagate across instances (shared model preferences, workflow tuning, etc). Instance-local settings remain in .automaker/settings.json. Effective config = proto.config defaults MERGED with shared CRDT settings MERGED with local overrides.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared settings document syncs across instances
Settings changed on any instance propagate to all peers
Instance-local overrides take precedence over shared settings
proto.config defaults < shared CRDT settings < local overrides resolution order
Credentials and API keys are NEVER included in shared settings

milestones.title: Cross-Instance Assignment
milestones.description: Enable features to flow between instances based on capacity, domain ownership, and priority. Work-stealing protocol for idle instances.

phases.name: instance-capacity-advertising
phases.title: Instance Capacity Advertising
phases.description: Each instance periodically updates its InstanceCapacity in the CRDT assignments document: running agents, max agents, RAM usage, CPU load, backlog depth. This data is used by the work-stealing protocol to identify idle instances and busy peers.
acceptanceCriteria: Each instance publishes capacity metrics to shared CRDT document
Capacity updates every heartbeat interval
Includes: runningAgents, maxAgents, backlogCount, ramUsagePercent, cpuPercent
All instances can read all peers' capacity in real-time
Health endpoint shows peer capacity summary

phases.name: work-stealing-protocol
phases.title: Work-Stealing Protocol
phases.description: When an instance's backlog empties, it requests features from busy peers. Assignment strategy configurable in proto.config: capacity (steal from busiest), domain (steal matching paths only), or manual (never auto-steal). Implements WORK_REQUEST/WORK_OFFER handshake via CRDT ephemeral messages. Feature.assignedInstance field update propagates via CRDT.
acceptanceCriteria: Idle instance broadcasts WORK_REQUEST when backlog hits 0
Busy peers respond with WORK_OFFER containing stealable features
Feature.assignedInstance updated via CRDT (propagates to all peers)
Stolen features appear in receiving instance's auto-mode queue
Domain routing: features only offered to instances whose domains match filesToModify
stealable:false features never offered
Configurable stealMax limits features stolen per cycle
Manual mode disables automatic stealing (features must be explicitly assigned)

phases.name: unified-dashboard-view
phases.title: Unified Dashboard View
phases.description: UI aggregates board state across all connected instances. Shows which instance owns each feature, peer status (online/offline/draining), capacity metrics per instance, and cross-instance assignment history. Leverages existing board view with instance badges and a new peers panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Board view shows instance badge on each feature card
Peers panel shows all connected instances with status and capacity
Features from all instances visible in unified board
Filter by instance (show only my features, show all)
Cross-instance assignment visible in feature detail view

milestones.title: Rich Text and Polish
milestones.description: Upgrade notes from workspace-level sync to character-level rich-text CRDT, and add operational polish for production multi-instance use.

phases.name: notes-rich-text-crdt
phases.title: Notes Rich-Text CRDT
phases.description: Replace workspace-level notes sync (HTML string LWW) with character-level collaborative editing via @automerge/prosemirror. Tiptap's ProseMirror layer wires to Automerge document for real-time co-editing. Multiple users on different instances can edit the same note tab simultaneously with conflict-free character merging.
acceptanceCriteria: @automerge/prosemirror integrated with Tiptap editor
Two users editing same note tab see each other's changes in real-time
Concurrent edits merge without conflicts
Cursor/selection awareness across instances (stretch goal)
Backward compatible: single-instance mode still works without CRDT

phases.name: operational-hardening
phases.title: Operational Hardening
phases.description: Production readiness: CRDT document compaction (prevent unbounded growth), sync conflict diagnostics, reconnection resilience, monitoring and alerting for sync health, and documentation for multi-instance deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Document compaction runs periodically to prevent unbounded history growth
Sync health metrics exposed via /api/health/detailed
Reconnection after network partition replays queued changes correctly
Alert when peer is unreachable for > peerTtlMs
Deployment guide for multi-instance setup on Tailscale
Runbook for common sync issues (split-brain, stuck peers, doc corruption)

milestones.title: Ava Orchestrator Mesh
milestones.description: Enable Ava orchestrators on each instance to communicate with each other — sharing status, requesting help, coordinating cross-instance work, and logging conversations. An MCP tool and UI view provide visibility into inter-Ava dialogue.

phases.name: ava-mesh-protocol
phases.title: Ava Mesh Communication Protocol
phases.description: Create a new CRDT document (doc:ava-mesh) that stores inter-Ava messages. Each message has sender instanceId, recipient (specific instance or broadcast), message type (status-update, help-request, work-handoff, escalation, heartbeat), content, and timestamp. Ava instances post to this document; all peers see the full conversation log. Messages use Automerge's append-only list CRDT for ordered history. Define cadence options: periodic check-in (configurable interval), event-driven (on feature completion, escalation, idle), or on-demand (MCP tool invocation).
acceptanceCriteria: AvaMeshMessage type defined with sender, recipient, messageType, content, timestamp, instanceId
Message types: status-update, help-request, help-response, work-handoff, escalation, coordination, heartbeat
doc:ava-mesh CRDT document syncs across all instances
Messages are append-only (grow-only list CRDT, no edits or deletes)
Configurable check-in cadence in proto.config.yaml (default: on feature completion + every 15 minutes)
AvaMeshService provides sendMessage(), getMessages(), getConversation() methods
Unit tests for message serialization and CRDT append

phases.name: ava-mesh-mcp-tools
phases.title: Ava Mesh MCP Tools
phases.description: Expose inter-Ava communication via MCP tools so Ava orchestrators (running as Claude Code sessions) can programmatically send messages to peers, request help, hand off work, and read the conversation log. Tools: send_mesh_message, list_mesh_messages, request_peer_help, broadcast_status. These tools are the API through which Ava personas on each instance interact with each other.
acceptanceCriteria: send_mesh_message MCP tool sends typed message to specific peer or broadcast
list_mesh_messages MCP tool returns recent messages with filtering (by sender, type, time range)
request_peer_help MCP tool sends help-request with context (feature details, error info, what's needed)
broadcast_status MCP tool sends status-update to all peers (board summary, capacity, current work)
Ava skill prompt updated to use mesh tools when hivemind is active
Server routes: POST /api/ava-mesh/send, GET /api/ava-mesh/messages
Messages from MCP tools propagate via CRDT to all connected instances

phases.name: ava-mesh-ui-view
phases.title: Ava Mesh Conversation View
phases.description: Add a UI view (accessible from the sidebar or peers panel) that displays the inter-Ava conversation log. Shows messages grouped by conversation thread or chronologically, with instance avatars/badges, message types color-coded, and real-time updates via WebSocket. Allows the human operator to observe how Ava instances coordinate, inject messages, and override decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: Ava Mesh view accessible from sidebar navigation
Messages displayed chronologically with instance badge and message type icon
Message types color-coded (status: blue, help-request: amber, escalation: red, handoff: green)
Real-time updates as new messages arrive via CRDT sync
Filter by instance, message type, or time range
Human operator can send messages into the mesh (operator override)
Conversation threads: related messages (request + response) visually grouped
Empty state when hivemind not active: shows setup instructions

milestones.title: Calendar and Todo Sync
milestones.description: Add Calendar (events) and Todo (lists) as shared CRDT domains. Todos have a three-tier permission model: user lists (agent-readable), per-instance Ava lists (writable only by owning Ava + user, readable by all Avas), and shared lists. Calendar events sync across all instances. These complete the data model hierarchy: Parent Project > Projects/Calendar/Notes/Todo.

phases.name: calendar-crdt-sync
phases.title: Calendar CRDT Sync
phases.description: Create Automerge document (doc:calendar) storing calendar events. Events sync across all instances so deadlines, ceremonies, and milestones are visible everywhere. Integrates with existing CalendarService and MCP calendar tools (create_calendar_event, list_calendar_events, etc). Events have standard fields: title, start, end, type (deadline, ceremony, milestone, custom), linkedProjectSlug, linkedFeatureId. Calendar is a shared resource — any instance can create/edit/delete events.
acceptanceCriteria: doc:calendar CRDT document syncs across all instances
Events created on any instance appear on all peers
Existing MCP calendar tools work through CRDT layer when hivemind active
Ceremony events auto-created when CeremonySchedulerService fires
Milestone/project deadline events sync with project data
Backward compatible: works without CRDT (falls back to filesystem)

phases.name: todo-data-model-and-permissions
phases.title: Todo Data Model and Permission System
phases.description: Create Todo types and storage. A TodoList has an owner (user or instanceId), a permission tier (user-private, ava-instance, shared), and contains TodoItems. Permission tiers: (1) user lists — user read/write, all Avas read-only. (2) ava-instance lists — writable by owning instance's Ava + user, readable by all Avas on all instances. (3) shared lists — full read/write by anyone. Each Ava instance automatically gets an ava-instance list on first boot. Course correction between Avas happens via mesh chat, not by writing to each other's todo lists.
acceptanceCriteria: TodoList type with id, name, ownerType (user|ava-instance|shared), ownerInstanceId, items[]
TodoItem type with id, title, description, completed, dueDate, priority, linkedFeatureId
Permission enforcement: ava-instance lists reject writes from non-owning instances
doc:todos CRDT document syncs across all instances
Each instance's Ava auto-creates its own ava-instance list on first activation
User can create user-private and shared lists
All Avas can read all lists (for situational awareness)
Unit tests for permission enforcement across tiers

phases.name: todo-mcp-tools-and-ui
phases.title: Todo MCP Tools and UI
phases.description: Expose todo operations via MCP tools and add a Todo view to the UI. MCP tools: create_todo_list, list_todo_lists, add_todo_item, update_todo_item, complete_todo_item, delete_todo_item. The UI shows all visible lists with permission badges, grouped by tier. Ava skill integration: Ava writes its planned work to its own todo list so the user and other Avas can see what it intends to do. Ava reads other instances' todo lists for awareness but coordinates via mesh chat.
acceptanceCriteria: MCP tools: create_todo_list, list_todo_lists, add_todo_item, update_todo_item, complete_todo_item
Permission enforcement in MCP tools (reject writes to non-owned ava-instance lists)
Todo view in sidebar showing all lists grouped by tier
Permission badges on lists (lock icon for read-only, instance badge for ava-instance)
Ava skill writes planned work items to its instance todo list on activation
Ava reads peer todo lists via list_todo_lists for cross-instance awareness
Real-time updates via CRDT sync (new items appear across instances)
Linked items: todo items can reference features, showing status inline

prd.situation: protoLabs Studio currently operates as a single-instance system where .automaker/ files (features, projects, notes, settings) are local to each machine. The FeatureStore interface, EventBus.broadcast(), and HivemindConfig types are already extracted as seams for multi-instance operation, but no sync layer exists. Each instance maintains its own board state independently. Knowledge (context, memory, skills) syncs via git, but operational state (features, projects) does not propagate between instances.
prd.problem: Users with multiple machines (dev laptop, staging VM, production node) or collaborators sharing compute cannot see or act on the same board state. Features created on one instance are invisible to others. Compute sits idle when one instance finishes its backlog while another is overloaded. There is no way to assign tickets across instances, share project ceremonies, or get a unified view of work in progress. This limits the system to single-machine scaling and prevents the hivemind architecture from materializing.
prd.approach: Implement a CRDT-based sync layer using Automerge 3 + automerge-repo over WebSocket on Tailscale VPN. Shared data is organized into Automerge documents by domain: features, projects, notes workspace, and shared settings. A proto.config.yaml file (git-tracked) defines shared defaults and instance registry with layered overrides. Sync topology is star with automatic leader election — one instance runs the automerge-repo-sync-server, others connect as WebSocket clients. Cross-instance feature assignment uses a work-stealing protocol where idle instances request features from busy peers, with CRDT field updates propagating assignments conflict-free. The implementation phases in through the existing FeatureStore and EventBus abstractions, ensuring zero breaking changes to callers. Instance-local data (agent output, trajectories, worktrees, credentials) stays local.
prd.results: Multiple Automaker instances share board state with sub-100ms propagation. Features created on any instance appear on all others within milliseconds. Cross-instance assignment enables work to flow to available compute automatically. Each instance operates offline-first and merges seamlessly on reconnect. A unified dashboard shows all instances' work. The proto.config.yaml provides shared configuration with per-instance overrides. New data domains can be added by creating new Automerge documents without architectural changes.
constraints: Must work over Tailscale VPN (stable IPs, encrypted transport, NAT traversal handled)
Must be offline-first — each instance works independently when disconnected
Must not break single-instance operation — CRDT layer is opt-in via proto.config.yaml
Must use existing FeatureStore and EventBus interfaces — no breaking changes to callers
Agent output, trajectories, worktrees, and credentials remain instance-local (never synced)
Knowledge layer (context, memory, skills) continues syncing via git — not duplicated in CRDT
Automerge 3 is the CRDT library (JSON-native, 10x memory improvement, automerge-repo networking)
Star topology with automatic leader election for 2-10 nodes
proto.config.yaml is git-tracked; instance-local overrides in .automaker/settings.json
Cross-instance assignment via work-stealing protocol, not centralized scheduler
Notes rich-text CRDT (via @automerge/prosemirror) is deferred to Phase 5 — Phase 3 syncs notes at the workspace JSON level
Ledger files (events.jsonl, completion-emitted.jsonl, ceremony-processed.jsonl) remain instance-local — append-only logs are not suitable for CRDT sync
PR tracking state (pr-tracking.json) remains instance-local — each instance owns its PRs
Calendar events (doc:calendar) and Todos (doc:todos) are future sync domains — add when those surfaces mature
M1 must include a bootstrap/migration phase: on first proto.config.yaml enable, hydrate CRDT documents from existing .automaker/ filesystem data
doc:assignments should be a separate CRDT document from doc:features — keeps work-stealing handshake messages and capacity data isolated
When a feature is reassigned via work-stealing, PR watermarks (ownership.instanceId) must be updated on the PR
